quizQuestion: In what year did Spider-Man make his comic book debut in Amazing Fantasy #15?
explanation: Spider-Man first appeared in Amazing Fantasy #15, published in 1962. Created by Stan Lee and Steve Ditko, the teenage hero with relatable personal problems became an instant hit with young readers. Source: Wikipedia/Spider-Man

quizQuestion: Which artist co-created Spider-Man alongside writer Stan Lee?
explanation: Steve Ditko co-created Spider-Man with Stan Lee, designing the character's costume and contributing key plot concepts. Ditko and Lee together crafted a teenage superhero whose personal struggles resonated deeply with young readers. Source: Wikipedia/Spider-Man

quizQuestion: The first issue of The X-Men was published in what year?
explanation: The X-Men debuted in The X-Men #1 in 1963, created by Stan Lee and Jack Kirby. Unlike other heroes, the mutant team was born with their powers, eliminating the need for separate origin stories for each member. Source: Wikipedia/X-Men

quizQuestion: Stan Lee co-created the X-Men with which legendary Marvel artist?
explanation: Stan Lee and Jack Kirby co-created the X-Men in 1963. The concept of mutants born with superhuman abilities — rather than acquiring powers through accidents — was an innovative storytelling device that defined the franchise for decades. Source: Wikipedia/X-Men

quizQuestion: Iron Man, Thor, Hulk, and Ant-Man were four of the five original Avengers — who was the fifth founding member?
explanation: The Wasp (Janet Van Dyne) was the fifth founding Avenger alongside Iron Man, Ant-Man, Thor, and Hulk when The Avengers #1 debuted in 1963. She is often overlooked but was present from the very beginning. Source: Wikipedia/Avengers

quizQuestion: The Avengers were created in 1963 partly as Marvel's response to which rival DC superhero team?
explanation: Stan Lee and Jack Kirby created the Avengers in 1963 largely as a response to DC's Justice League of America. Marvel publisher Martin Goodman reportedly instructed Lee to create a team book after hearing about JLA's strong sales. Source: Wikipedia/Avengers

quizQuestion: In which 1966 comic book issue did Black Panther make his debut as the first Black superhero in American mainstream comics?
explanation: Black Panther first appeared in Fantastic Four #52 in 1966, created by Stan Lee and Jack Kirby. Notably, this debut predated the formation of the Black Panther Party political organization, despite the shared name. Source: Wikipedia/Black Panther

quizQuestion: In what year did Black Panther debut as the first Black superhero in American mainstream comics?
explanation: Black Panther debuted in Fantastic Four #52 in 1966, created by Stan Lee and Jack Kirby. His arrival predated the Black Panther Party political organization, making the name coincidence a source of frequent historical confusion. Source: Wikipedia/Black Panther

quizQuestion: Jack Kirby co-created Captain America with which other writer-artist in 1941?
explanation: Joe Simon and Jack Kirby co-created Captain America Comics #1 in 1941. The cover famously depicted Captain America punching Adolf Hitler — a bold statement published months before the United States entered World War II. Source: Wikipedia/Captain America

quizQuestion: Captain America Comics #1 — which showed the hero punching Hitler on its cover — was published in what year?
explanation: Captain America Comics #1 debuted in 1941, created by Joe Simon and Jack Kirby. The issue sold approximately one million copies and appeared months before the United States officially entered World War II after Pearl Harbor. Source: Wikipedia/Captain America

quizQuestion: In which year did Superman first appear in Action Comics #1, establishing the superhero genre?
explanation: Superman debuted in Action Comics #1 in 1938, created by Jerry Siegel and Joe Shuster. The character effectively invented the superhero genre, and the creators infamously sold the copyright for just $130. Source: Wikipedia/Superman

quizQuestion: Joe Shuster drew Superman, but who wrote the character and co-created him?
explanation: Jerry Siegel and Joe Shuster co-created Superman, who debuted in Action Comics #1 in 1938. The pair sold the copyright for $130 — a decision that became one of the most debated creator rights stories in comic book history. Source: Wikipedia/Superman

quizQuestion: In which comic book issue did Batman make his first appearance in 1939?
explanation: Batman first appeared in Detective Comics #27 in 1939, created by Bob Kane with crucial contributions from Bill Finger. The 'DC' in DC Comics actually stands for 'Detective Comics,' the anthology series where Batman debuted. Source: Wikipedia/Batman

quizQuestion: Despite designing Batman's iconic cowl, cape, and gloves and writing many early stories, Bill Finger was not officially credited as a co-creator until what year?
explanation: Bill Finger received official co-creator credit from DC Comics/Warner Bros. in 2015, more than 75 years after Batman's debut. Finger designed the costume and wrote key stories, while Bob Kane originally received sole credit. Source: Wikipedia/Batman

quizQuestion: Wonder Woman was created by a psychologist who wanted her to embody triumph through love — what was his name?
explanation: William Moulton Marston, a psychologist who also helped develop the lie detector test, created Wonder Woman in 1941. He deliberately designed her to triumph through love and compassion rather than violence, making her thematically distinct from male heroes. Source: Wikipedia/Wonder Woman

quizQuestion: Wonder Woman made her debut in All Star Comics #8 in what year?
explanation: Wonder Woman first appeared in All Star Comics #8 in 1941, created by psychologist William Moulton Marston. She was designed as a feminist icon who would defeat enemies through love rather than brute force. Source: Wikipedia/Wonder Woman

quizQuestion: Dave Gibbons drew Watchmen — which British writer authored the groundbreaking 12-issue limited series?
explanation: Alan Moore wrote Watchmen, illustrated by Dave Gibbons, published from 1986 to 1987. The series fundamentally deconstructed the superhero genre and was later included in Time magazine's list of the 100 best English-language novels. Source: Wikipedia/Watchmen

quizQuestion: Watchmen by Alan Moore and Dave Gibbons helped popularize what term for a self-contained book-length comic narrative?
explanation: Watchmen helped mainstream the term 'graphic novel' for an extended, self-contained comic narrative intended as serious literature. Originally published as a 12-issue limited series from 1986-1987, it became one of the defining examples of the format. Source: Wikipedia/Watchmen

quizQuestion: Robert Kirkman's The Walking Dead was published by which independent comics company known for creator-owned titles?
explanation: The Walking Dead was published by Image Comics starting in 2003, created by Robert Kirkman and artist Tony Moore. Image Comics was founded in 1992 specifically to allow artists and writers to retain ownership of their work. Source: Wikipedia/The Walking Dead

quizQuestion: How many total issues did Robert Kirkman's The Walking Dead comic series run before its surprise 2019 ending?
explanation: The Walking Dead ran for 193 issues before Robert Kirkman ended it without warning in July 2019. Kirkman stated he ended the series abruptly to preserve its integrity and avoid a slow decline — the final issue shipped with no advance announcement. Source: Wikipedia/The Walking Dead

quizQuestion: Which former Spider-Man artist created Spawn for Image Comics in 1992?
explanation: Todd McFarlane created Spawn in 1992, published by Image Comics. Issue #1 sold 1.7 million copies, setting a record for an independent comic book. McFarlane had previously become famous for his acclaimed run as artist on The Amazing Spider-Man. Source: Wikipedia/Spawn

quizQuestion: Spawn #1 set a record for independent comics by selling approximately how many copies?
explanation: Spawn #1 sold approximately 1.7 million copies when it debuted in 1992, setting a record for an independently published comic book. Creator Todd McFarlane had been one of Marvel's top-selling Spider-Man artists before leaving to found Image Comics. Source: Wikipedia/Spawn

quizQuestion: Peter Laird co-created the Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles with which artist partner in 1984?
explanation: Kevin Eastman and Peter Laird created the Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles in 1984 for Mirage Studios. Originally conceived as a parody of Frank Miller's Daredevil and Chris Claremont's X-Men, the first print run was just 3,275 copies. Source: Wikipedia/TMNT

quizQuestion: The first issue of Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles had an initial print run of only how many copies?
explanation: The first Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles issue had an initial print run of just 3,275 copies in 1984. Despite these humble origins as a parody, the franchise exploded into one of the most successful entertainment properties of the 1980s and 1990s. Source: Wikipedia/TMNT

quizQuestion: Senate hearings on comics' impact on youth led to the formation of the Comics Code Authority in what year?
explanation: The Comics Code Authority was established in 1954 following Senate subcommittee hearings that examined whether comic books contributed to juvenile delinquency. It imposed 41 content restrictions and effectively drove EC Comics — known for horror titles — out of business. Source: Wikipedia/Comics Code Authority

quizQuestion: When founded in 1954, how many content restrictions did the Comics Code Authority impose on publishers?
explanation: The Comics Code Authority imposed 41 content restrictions covering violence, sexual content, and depictions of authority figures when it was established in 1954. It remained in force until 2011, when the last publishers abandoned it. Source: Wikipedia/Comics Code Authority

quizQuestion: Stan Lee co-created Spider-Man, X-Men, Iron Man, Thor, and Hulk — which superhero team also belongs to his creations?
explanation: Stan Lee co-created the Fantastic Four alongside Jack Kirby, along with Spider-Man, X-Men, Iron Man, Thor, and the Hulk. Lee received the National Medal of Arts and became famous for cameo appearances in Marvel films. Source: Wikipedia/Stan Lee

quizQuestion: Stan Lee, who passed away in 2018, was born in what year?
explanation: Stan Lee was born in 1922 and died in 2018 at the age of 95. He spent his career at Marvel Comics, co-creating some of the most enduring superhero characters in pop culture history and serving as Marvel's creative leader for decades. Source: Wikipedia/Stan Lee

quizQuestion: Jack Kirby co-created Captain America, the Fantastic Four, the X-Men, and Black Panther — what celebrated nickname did he earn?
explanation: Jack Kirby earned the nickname 'King of Comics' for his unparalleled influence on the medium. Born in 1917, he co-created some of Marvel's most iconic characters alongside Stan Lee and Joe Simon, shaping the visual language of superhero comics for generations. Source: Wikipedia/Jack Kirby

quizQuestion: Jack Kirby, who died in 1994, was born in what year?
explanation: Jack Kirby was born in 1917 and died in 1994 at the age of 76. Nicknamed the 'King of Comics,' he co-created Captain America in 1941 and continued to define superhero comics for more than 50 years. Source: Wikipedia/Jack Kirby
